characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Book Dumb: The highest GPA that we know of a football player has had was a B+ (Craig Shilo in "Midterms"). In addition, they all have their own nerds doing their academic work for them, eventually leading to a boycott in the episode "Nerds" after Sammy accidentally puts one of the nerds in a coma. Dumb Jock: They're not the sharpest knives in the drawer. Frat Bro: They all fit the stereotype to some degree. Jerk Jock: Although theyarethe focus of the show, this doesn't mean that they're very nice people. The show doesn't really focus on their treatment of people lower on the social hierarchy than them, other than their personal nerds forRule of Funny. Karma Houdini Warranty: Their antics cause huge damages to the school, involve many illegal acts, and violate various NCAA rules. They seem to get away with ituntil the episode "Death Penalty".

characters.name: As a Whole
characters.description: Book Dumb: The highest GPA that we know of a football player has had was a B+ (Craig Shilo in "Midterms"). In addition, they all have their own nerds doing their academic work for them, eventually leading to a boycott in the episode "Nerds" after Sammy accidentally puts one of the nerds in a coma. Dumb Jock: They're not the sharpest knives in the drawer. Frat Bro: They all fit the stereotype to some degree. Jerk Jock: Although theyarethe focus of the show, this doesn't mean that they're very nice people. The show doesn't really focus on their treatment of people lower on the social hierarchy than them, other than their personal nerds forRule of Funny. Karma Houdini Warranty: Their antics cause huge damages to the school, involve many illegal acts, and violate various NCAA rules. They seem to get away with ituntil the episode "Death Penalty".

characters.name: Alex Moran
characters.description: The Second String Quarterback, later becoming the Starting Quarterback of the team. Brilliant, but Lazy: He's actually pretty smart, but does not really want to put in the effort, preferring to let the nerds do his homework while he gets drunk and/or laid. He's also a competent, talented quarterback, but likes being a second-stringer who doesn't get knocked around as much. When the coach makes him a starter, Alex works his ass off to get himself bumped back down to the bench. He startschangingin Season 3 though.Backup quarterback is the best position in sports! It's like starting quarterback, only without the pain! The Casanova: Most of the football team is popular with the ladies but Moran is most popular with them. Delayed Leadership Ascendance: Moran made no serious effort to take the starting position until his junior year, and even then it took Thad getting suspended and Debra putting his head straight to convince him. Deadpan Snarker: He comments a lot on the insane shenanigans of the school.

characters.name: Sammy Cacciatore
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: Nothing ever goes right for him. Lovable Sex Maniac: He's pretty likable despite his libido. Mascot Breaks Character: Sammy plays Billy the Mountain Goat, and - even in full goat costume - tends to speak to anyone who interacts with him, whether it be the spirit squad, the football team, or even students and visitors to the BMS campus. My Sister Is Off-Limits: Inverted. Sammy is more than happy about his best friend dating his half-sister and even suspects the two will be married. The Team Wannabe: Sammy believes being the mascot entitles him to living the same high life as the football team. It takes Radon (who has befriended the nerds) to make Sammy realize he's no jock. He points out he's small, has no athletic abilities outside being the mascot, is not a chick magnet, does his own homework, is not concerned with his personal hygiene, has no sense of fashion and to top it all off, the BMS captains sold him out to the nerds as the one who put their friend in a coma.

characters.name: Thad Castle
characters.description: The Team Captain and Linebacker of the Mountain Goats. Ax-Crazy: Thad is completely and utterlynuts. Big Brother Instinct: Naturally to his little sister Alma.Extends to the team as well, as Thad will tolerate no offense to any of his teammates from anyone who's not a football player. Even if the teammate getting pranked/threatened/assaulted/insulted is somebody he normally treats like crap. Extends to the team as well, as Thad will tolerate no offense to any of his teammates from anyone who's not a football player. Even if the teammate getting pranked/threatened/assaulted/insulted is somebody he normally treats like crap. Bunny-Ears Lawyer: This guy is a savant at football to the point where he made captain as a sophomore. Also, his utterly bizarre ideas when it comes to football (such as the infamous oil change)dowork and shows he has a good grasp of how the team is. However, he is colossally insane. Alex puts it best in the movie: "Really good football player. Really horrible person."

characters.name: Craig Shilo
characters.description: The Starting Running Back. Back for the Finale: He comes back for the film and helps out Alex. Butt-Monkey: For much of season 1, nothing went right for him.It's telling that things turned around for him as soon as he dumped Denise. Only Sane Man: Compared toThe SlackerAlex, theAx-CrazyThad,ManchildSammy, and the rest of the football team, he's much more reasonable and tries to avoid the worst of the shenanigans. Put on a Bus: After Season 1, he leaves Blue Mountain State and transfers to Georgia Tech. Justified, as he probably didn't feel an attachment to the place anymore after dealing with everyone's antics andfinding out his girlfriend was cheating on him.

characters.name: Radon Randell
characters.description: The Starter after Craig leaves. Dark and Troubled Past:Played for Laughs. He grew up dividing his time between the inner city and juvenile detention centers. Glass Cannon: While he's a talented player, he's also injury prone.Eventually he has to retire from football entirely. Jerk with a Heart of Gold: He's very egotistical and can be a massive asshole, but he does have his positive moments. Notably, he quickly befriends his nerd and supports them in their campaign against the football team.

characters.name: Larry Summers
characters.description: Ascended Extra: He starts of as aSatellite Characterto Thad, but starts hanging out with the other players in the third season. No-Respect Guy: In the movie, he's become a graduate assistant coach. Unfortunately, none of the players treat him as a superior because they still see him as their friend/teammate. The Quiet One: In some episodes, he doesn't speak at all.

characters.name: Donald "Donnie" Schrab
characters.description: Ascended Extra: Starts Season 1 on special teams (assuming he a bit ofTruth in Television

characters.name: Harmon Tedesco
characters.description: A Day in the Limelight: The plot of "Superstition" is set around Harmon and his attempts to overcome the yips. Hidden Depths: Moran walks in on him...painting a remarkably good classical rendition of St Basil’s Cathedral.From memory. Only Sane Man: Less so than Moran, but he is often the voice of reason (or at least lesser stupidity). The Stoner: He is by far the biggest drug user on the team. Southern-Fried Genius: He has a thick Southern accent, but is a skilled chemist and, as mentioned above, a shockingly good artist.

characters.name: Coach Martin "Marty" Daniels
characters.description: Cool Old Guy: While he's strict with the players, he's also a former NFL star who has lived the high life. He and Alex wind up bonding over tales of their exploits. Not So Above It All: Normally, he's frustrated by his players' off the field antics and reminds them that their college days are going to end very soon. However, there are times when he gets caught up in the craziness with them, such as when he learns about the prank war with the lacrosse team and orders Thad to do what is necessary to get back at them for burying Alex in the practice field. You Are Better Than You Think You Are: Gives Alex one of these speeches to motivate him into pushing himself to live up to his potential.

characters.name: Denise Roy
characters.description: Craig's girlfriend since high school. Jerkass: She treats Craig like dirt most of the time.Even worse, it's revealed she cheated on him after forcing him to stay abstinent. The Tease: She will not let Craig have sex with her under the pretense of making him keep all the testosterone he would be expending on sex into football instead.

characters.name: Mary Jo Cacciatore
characters.description: Sammy's younger sister who joins the cheerleader squad. Abhorrent Admirer: To Alex. While different in that she is exceptionally attractive, she's also the younger sister of his best friend, whom Alex doesn't want to hurt.They eventually hook up at the end of Season 2, but Sammy is not only fine with it, but ecstatic. Ambiguously Bi: Spends most of Season 2 chasing after Alex. Starts ignoring him in Season 3 in favor of girls.Eventually tells Alex the truth in The Rise of Thadland. Brilliant, but Lazy: Entered BMS on a full academic scholarship, is in Pre-Med, but doesn't attend a single class because she'd rather drink and party. Foolish Sibling, Responsible Sibling: She's more level-headed than Sammy, though that's not really a high bar to clear.

characters.name: Dean Simon
characters.description: Dean Bitterman: He hates that people only care about the BMS football team and partying and not the academics. Jerkass Has a Point: He's highly antagonistic against the Goats.... because they're a bunch of drunk, horny, destructive jerks who bring the university's reputation and credibility as an academic institution down.
